etymology.description: 孫(孫) (sūn, "grandchild") + 子(子) (zǐ, "child; master (polite form)") refers to a person's descendants or is used as an honorific for notable figures. In this context, 孫子 (Sūnzǐ) commonly refers to the famed military strategist Sun Tzu, author of "The Art of War."
etymology.interpretation: 孫(孫) (sūn, "grandchild") denotes a descendant or successor, linking to one's lineage, while 子(子) (zǐ, "child; master") often implies respect. Hence, 孫子 (Sūnzǐ) ascribes him not only by his familial lineage but also acknowledges his status as a masterly figure.

examples.chinese: 孙子(孫子)是著名的军事家(是著名的軍事家)。
examples.english: Sun Tzu is a famous military strategist.

examples.chinese: 《孙子兵法》(《孫子兵法》)是一部经典的军事著作(是一部經典的軍事著作)。
examples.pinyin: 《Sūnzǐ Bīngfǎ》 shì yī bù jīngdiǎn de jūnshì zhùzuò.
examples.english: "The Art of War" by Sun Tzu is a classic military book.

examples.chinese: 孙子的智慧影响了后世的策略家(孫子的智慧影響了後世的策略家)。
examples.pinyin: Sūnzǐ de zhìhuì yǐngxiǎngle hòushì de cèlüèjiā.
examples.english: Sun Tzu's wisdom has influenced strategists of later generations.
